title: Iliad, Book 21: The Battle at the River
description: Book 21 of Homer's Iliad: The Battle at the River.
introduction: Book 21 of the Iliad: The Battle at the River. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Ἀλλ᾿ ὅτε δὴ πόρον ἷξον ἐϋρρεῖος ποταμοῖο.
translation.text: Achilles drove the Trojans into the river Scamander and slaughtered them in the water until the river god rose in fury and tried to drown him. Hephaestus burned the river back. The gods fought each other — Athena struck Ares down, Hera boxed Artemis' ears.
